title: Theres The Rub 2

jokes.text: It's an honor, Your Honor.
jokes.setup: Police officer addressing a judge formally
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'honor' as both respect and the judge's title
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that works but is predictable and somewhat clichéd

jokes.text: Well, we can't say nothing happened, Uncle Phil. You know, there was a couple of butt-naked honeys scattered around that place.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil trying to downplay their predicament
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt acknowledgment of the compromising situation
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Will's street-smart honesty contrasts with Phil's denial

jokes.text: I guess you wasn't at the Million Man March, huh?
jokes.setup: Black police officer treating them poorly
jokes.punchline: Will invoking racial solidarity to shame the officer
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Will's characteristic wit and timing

jokes.text: Oh, girl, that boy couldn't keep up with me if I gave him a head start.
jokes.setup: Talk about Will going to Thanksgiving parties
jokes.punchline: Vivian boasting about her own party stamina
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Vivian's confident, playful side

jokes.text: Girl, that man wasn't looking at you, he was looking at me. Looking at me like it was lunchtime and my legs was Colonel Sanders' extra crispy.
jokes.setup: Competition over a delivery man's attention
jokes.punchline: Vivian's colorful food metaphor for sexual attraction
jokes.explanation: Creative and memorable metaphor that's both funny and slightly risqué

jokes.text: If you mean something fast and cheap, yes.
jokes.setup: Asked if the delivery man was looking at the women lustfully
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry interpretation deflating their vanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - economical, character-specific, and perfectly timed

jokes.text: Will you be my friend?
jokes.setup: Intimidating large cellmate approaching Will and Phil
jokes.punchline: Childlike, innocent request instead of threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - completely defuses tension with innocent vulnerability

jokes.text: Um... No.
jokes.setup: Skippy's innocent friendship request
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt, uncomfortable rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Will's honest discomfort with the situation

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you might know your way around the mean streets of Bel-Air... but I think you better let me handle this brother on the phone, all right?
jokes.setup: Will offering to handle phone negotiations
jokes.punchline: Mocking Bel-Air as 'mean streets' while asserting street cred
jokes.explanation: Classic Will humor - mocking his privileged environment while asserting his street credentials

jokes.text: What's the big deal anyway? It wouldn't be the first time you broke somebody's legs. All right. I love you too, Mama.
jokes.setup: Threatening conversation about breaking legs
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking sweetly to his mother
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - tough guy persona immediately undercut by tender mother conversation

jokes.text: Maybe I don't come to places like this and do this kind of work as often as I could... but that's who I am, damn it. It doesn't make me a bad person.
jokes.setup: Hilary defending herself after being criticized
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of privilege delivered with misplaced defiance
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Hilary's oblivious privilege on full display with unintentional comedy

jokes.text: Honey, I live here.
jokes.setup: Hilary assuming woman is paid staff
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's actually a homeless resident
jokes.explanation: Effective reality check moment that punctures Hilary's assumptions

jokes.text: So the plates go in the back?
jokes.setup: Hilary's embarrassing realization about her mistake
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to move past the uncomfortable moment
jokes.explanation: Nice awkward beat showing Hilary trying to recover from her faux pas

jokes.text: I'm thinking that in a week when we look back on this, we are just gonna laugh. Maybe two weeks.
jokes.setup: Trying to stay optimistic about their jail situation
jokes.punchline: Extending the timeline, acknowledging it's worse than initially thought
jokes.explanation: Solid joke structure with Will's characteristic optimism tinged with realism

jokes.text: Hey, it's a good thing, man... I thought we was gonna have to Bruce Lee you up in here, you know?
jokes.setup: Discovering Skippy and Phil are old friends
jokes.punchline: Will's relief expressed through martial arts reference
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but relies on predictable pop culture reference

jokes.text: A shot to the helmet? Oh...
jokes.setup: Discussing an old football injury
jokes.punchline: Skippy's delayed realization connecting head trauma to current mental state
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that explains character's childlike behavior through tragic backstory

jokes.text: How about you, Banks? About the same.
jokes.setup: Skippy's tragic life story of injury, prison, and homelessness
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically comparing his privileged life to Skippy's hardships
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery highlighting the absurd contrast between their life experiences

jokes.text: Right, Mr. 'Hey, Big Guy... can I have a copy of your will in case something happens to you?'
jokes.setup: Carlton calling Hilary a taker
jokes.punchline: Hilary revealing Carlton's morbid financial planning
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific comeback that reveals Carlton's calculating nature

jokes.text: Yeah, if your last name's Menendez.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending his will request as 'prudent planning'
jokes.punchline: Reference to the infamous Menendez brothers murder case
jokes.explanation: Sharp, topical reference that darkly implies Carlton might kill Phil for inheritance

jokes.text: See, you're a nag. That's exactly why you don't have a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Escalating argument between siblings
jokes.punchline: Carlton's petty personal attack about Hilary's love life
jokes.explanation: Typical sibling meanness but lacks creativity or wit

jokes.text: Well, maybe they pulled in, smelled your stuffing and turned back around.
jokes.setup: Wondering why Phil and Will are late for dinner
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry suggestion that the cooking is driving them away
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey snark, though fairly predictable household humor

jokes.text: Shut up, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley starting a feminist rant about men
jokes.punchline: Vivian's abrupt shutdown of the lecture
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective family dynamic humor - cutting off pretentious teenager

jokes.text: There's a bug on mine.
jokes.setup: Prison food being served
jokes.punchline: Will's disgusted observation about the meal quality
jokes.explanation: Standard prison food joke - functional but uninspired

jokes.text: Stuck in a jail cell with some angry 400-pound man whose life you wrecked.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about their situation
jokes.punchline: Blaming Phil for Skippy's life while exaggerating his size and anger
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Will's tendency to exaggerate and blame others

jokes.text: And thank you so much for sharing it with me.
jokes.setup: Will calling it the worst Thanksgiving ever
jokes.punchline: Skippy's polite sarcasm about being blamed for their troubles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skippy's gentle nature despite being blamed

jokes.text: I'm about to dig in to this cream-of-bug sandwich.
jokes.setup: Horrible prison food situation
jokes.punchline: Will's creative description of bug-infested food as a soup variety
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that makes disgusting prison food sound like fancy soup

jokes.text: Big gorgeous roasted turkey... four different kinds of stuffing. Candied yams, and macaroni and cheese, mashed potatoes... And little tiny onions swimming in a sea of cream sauce.
jokes.setup: Describing what their family is eating while they're in jail
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate description of feast they're missing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building their misery, though runs a bit long

jokes.text: And we want $10,000 in unmarked bills... and a helicopter on the roof gassed up and ready to take us to Tijuana. Oh, sorry. I just kind of got caught up in the moment.
jokes.setup: Phil's authoritative demand for their release
jokes.punchline: Will adding ridiculous hostage movie demands then apologizing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection - Will getting carried away with movie fantasies

jokes.text: Grape jelly? You can't make these with no damn grape jelly.
jokes.setup: Prison food service
jokes.punchline: Prisoner's outrage about jelly variety in jail sandwiches
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities showing even in jail, people have food preferences

jokes.text: A turkey? Hardly. In point of fact, it's a Cornish game hen. A flightless bird hale from the Isle of Wight. Braised in cognac with a hint of fennel. A fitting end for a noble fowl.
jokes.setup: Being offered turkey at the shelter
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pretentious over-description of simple food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton pretentiousness - overwrought description of humble shelter food

jokes.text: You're kind of weird. So you want one or not?
jokes.setup: Carlton's elaborate food description
jokes.punchline: Practical response cutting through his pretension
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment deflating Carlton's pompousness with simple truth

jokes.text: I exploit people every day of the week on my show. It's Thanksgiving, I'm gonna take the day off.
jokes.setup: News crew wanting to film her charity work
jokes.punchline: Hilary's self-aware admission of her exploitative TV work
jokes.explanation: Excellent character growth moment - Hilary showing unexpected self-awareness and decency

jokes.text: For the first time in my life, I thought about others instead of myself. Ooh. I'm tingling all over.
jokes.setup: Hilary reflecting on her charitable behavior
jokes.punchline: Her narcissistic excitement about not being narcissistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - even her altruism becomes about her own feelings

jokes.text: Yeah. Just tell your boy he'd better be at the next march.
jokes.setup: Officer apologizing to Phil
jokes.punchline: Skippy following up on Will's Million Man March reference
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Skippy absorbed Will's lesson about racial solidarity

jokes.text: When you have a chronic back problem and you go for a therapeutic massage... and wind up in a house of ill-repute, which is subsequently raided... and you are arrested by mistake... never tell your wife. Hmm? Poof.
jokes.setup: Will asking what to tell Aunt Viv
jokes.punchline: Phil's elaborate euphemistic cover story with magic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's elaborate justifications and the 'poof' gesture is perfect

jokes.text: Of course, I stepped out of the way so they couldn't actually touch me... but I really connected.
jokes.setup: Hilary claiming to have connected with shelter residents
jokes.punchline: Admitting she avoided physical contact while claiming connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary contradiction - claiming emotional connection while admitting physical avoidance

jokes.text: Thank you for keeping us safe and delivering us from... Tiny. Tiny, as well as larger temptations.
jokes.setup: Thanksgiving prayer thanking God for protection
jokes.punchline: Awkward reference to Skippy and play on 'larger' temptations
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting their jail experience to traditional prayer language

jokes.text: Thank you for our health. Thank you for this food. But most of all, thank you for keeping us safe and delivering us from... Tiny. Tiny, as well as larger temptations.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil saying Thanksgiving grace
jokes.punchline: References Skippy (Tiny) and makes pun about 'larger temptations'
jokes.explanation: Clever way to reference their jail experience in prayer, good wordplay on size

jokes.text: A-woman.
jokes.setup: Traditional 'Amen' ending to prayer
jokes.punchline: Hilary's feminist alternative to 'Amen'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that fits Hilary's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Everything was exactly as delicious as everything else.
jokes.setup: Being pressed to judge between competing stuffing recipes
jokes.punchline: Phil's diplomatic non-answer avoiding family conflict
jokes.explanation: Clever diplomatic response that says nothing while sounding positive

jokes.text: I guess it was kind of fun when Vy dropped that bag of powdered sugar all over Helen.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on kitchen mishaps during cooking
jokes.punchline: Recalling slapstick accident with powdered sugar
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reference - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Yeah, talk about being led around by your apron strings.
jokes.setup: Vivian getting caught in food processor
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'apron strings' idiom becoming literal
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on literal vs metaphorical 'apron strings'

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah. Listen, I love y'all too... but the bottom line is my stuffing was the best. Any fool can see that. You tasted it. You tasted it with your own mouth.
jokes.setup: Family bonding moment about cooking together
jokes.punchline: Vivian's competitive insistence her stuffing was best
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing competitive nature breaking through family sentiment

tmdb_overview: Will and Philip are arrested at a massage parlor on Thanksgiving. Hilary and Carlton get a lesson in humility at the homeless shelter.
